title: 676. Implement Magic Dictionary
question:
Implement a magic directory with buildDict, and search methods.

For the method buildDict, you'll be given a list of non-repetitive words to build a dictionary.

For the method search, you'll be given a word, and judge whether if you modify exactly one character into another character in this word, the modified word is in the dictionary you just built.

Example 1:

Input: buildDict(["hello", "leetcode"]), Output: Null
Input: search("hello"), Output: False
Input: search("hhllo"), Output: True
Input: search("hell"), Output: False
Input: search("leetcoded"), Output: False

Note:

  1. You may assume that all the inputs are consist of lowercase letters a-z.
  2. For contest purpose, the test data is rather small by now. You could think about highly efficient algorithm after the contest.
  3. Please remember to RESET your class variables declared in class MagicDictionary, as static/class variables are persisted across multiple test cases. Please see here for more details.
code: class MagicDictionary(object):\u000A\u000A    # trie based\u000A    def ___init__(self):\u000A        self.dict \u003D {}\u000A\u000A    def _buildDict(self, dict):\u000A        for word in dict:\u000A            cur \u003D self.dict\u000A            for char in word: cur \u003D cur.setdefault(char, {})\u000A            cur[\u0022#\u0022] \u003D True\u000A\u000A    def _search(self, word):\u000A\u000A        def _cmp(i, cur):\u000A            for j in range(i, len(word)):\u000A                char \u003D word[j]\u000A                if char not in cur: return False\u000A                cur \u003D cur[char]\u000A            return \u0027#\u0027 in cur\u000A\u000A        cur \u003D self.dict\u000A        for i, char in enumerate(word):\u000A            for dic_char in cur:\u000A                if char !\u003D dic_char and dic_char !\u003D \u0027#\u0027:\u000A                    if _cmp(i + 1, cur[dic_char]): return True\u000A            if char not in cur: return False\u000A            cur \u003D cur[char]\u000A        return False\u000A    \u000A    # hash based\u000A    def __init__(self):\u000A        self.words \u003D {}\u000A\u000A    def buildDict(self, dict):\u000A        for word in dict:\u000A            for i in range(len(word)):\u000A                self.words.setdefault(word[:i] + \u0027_\u0027 + word[i + 1:], set()).add(word[i])\u000A        \u000A    def search(self, word):\u000A        for i, char in enumerate(word):\u000A            adj \u003D self.words.get(word[:i] + \u0027_\u0027 + word[i + 1:], None)\u000A            if adj is not None and adj \u002D set(char): return True\u000A        return False
